name: NYC Zoning Resolution — official text portal (zoningresolution.planning.nyc.gov)
source_type: official_html_portal (PRD section-8 tier 4; no API/SODA/structured-download channel exists for the ZR text — verified 2026-07-16)

authentication.evidence: All pages fetched anonymously live 2026-07-16 (E3, E8, E9, E13); no 403s from this host, unlike www.nyc.gov

rate_limits.observed: No throttling observed at research-scale fetch rates; platform is Drupal 9 on Pantheon behind Fastly/Varnish and returned 504 Gateway Timeout for on-the-fly PDF generation of the giant 12-10 definitions node (E10) - crawlers must be low-rate and resumable
rate_limits.evidence: Response headers X-Pantheon-Styx-Hostname / Via: 1.1 varnish (E4, E6)

update_frequency: Per adopted text amendment (event-driven, not scheduled). Homepage currency banner verbatim (raw HTML, E3): "All text changes approved by the city council as of May 20, 2026". Official disclaimer verbatim (E12): "CHANGES ARE MADE PERIODICALLY TO THE ZR AND THESE CHANGES MAY OR MAY NOT BE IMMEDIATELY REFLECTED IN THE MATERIALS LOCATED AT OR INFORMATION PRESENT ON HTTPS://ZR.PLANNING.NYC.GOV." Dated complete-PDF snapshots on /zr-downloads appear at an irregular 3-6 month cadence (10 snapshots 2024-03-22 through 2026-06-23, E8).
geographic_coverage: All five boroughs (citywide law). Homepage verbatim: "14 Articles and 11 Appendices, plus 126 Zoning Maps, that establish the zoning districts for the City and the regulations governing land use and development." (E3)

fields_available.note: Not a dataset; a structured legal-text corpus served as server-rendered Drupal 9 HTML (X-Generator header, E4). Structure verified live 2026-07-16.
structure: Articles I-XIV at /article-{roman} (titles: I General Provisions; II Residence District Regulations; III Commercial District Regulations; IV Manufacturing District Regulations; V Non-Conforming Uses and Non-Complying Buildings; VI Special Regulations Applicable to Certain Areas; VII Administration; VIII-XIV Special Purpose Districts) (E3, E12)
Chapters at /article-{roman}/chapter-{n}; chapter pages carry full text of all sections with #CC-NN anchors; chapter gaps exist (Article I has chapters 1,2,3,5,6 - no 4; OQ-5) (E8, E9)
Sections at /article-{roman}/chapter-{n}/{CC-NN[N[N]]}; per-section Drupal nodes (11-02 = node 18416; 12-10 = node 18523) (E9)
Section numbering: 2-digit prefix = article+chapter ordinals (11-xx, 12-xx, 74-xx); special-purpose-district chapters use 3-digit prefixes from 101-00 (official basis: section 12-01(j), E13)
Per-section machine-readable amendment stamp: <time datetime="1961-12-15T12:00:00Z" class="datetime">12/15/1961</time> after 'Last Amended' label (E9)

Defined terms rendered as <em> elements in body text, mapping to the 12-10 alphabetical definitions (which carry per-definition Last Amended dates, applicability notes, and FROM <section> attributions) (E9, E13)
Appendices B-K at descriptive aliases (Appendix F = parent + 5 borough pages; no Appendix A - /appendix-a 404, count reconciliation OQ-1); Preamble at /node/79; Disclaimer at /disclaimer (E12, E14)

Recently-adopted amendment feed at /recently-adopted (paginated; [CORRECTED AT G1 - C5] depth = 31 pages, ?page=0..30, per the raw 'Go to last page' pager href): entries = project name + application number (e.g. N 240272 ZRK), date, affected-section reference; stub pages without amendment text (E11; G1 report 1.9)

[CORRECTED AT G1 - C1] Per-section amendment-history AJAX endpoint (undocumented; resolves OQ-9): markup carries href /nojs/get/amendment/section/{sectionEntityId} (class use-ajax action-icon revisions; plain GET -> 404); working form GET /ajax/get/amendment/section/{id}?_wrapper_format=drupal_ajax with header X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest -> 200 application/json, a Drupal AJAX command array whose insert payload is the amendment_history_popup view: 6-column table Effective Date | ULURP/CPC Report | Project Name | Action | Notes | Description (verified for section 11-47, sectionEntityId 22740: 12/5/2024 | N240290ZRY | City of Yes for Housing Opportunity | Added). Section-entity IDs are a THIRD identifier namespace distinct from Drupal node IDs and section numbers. Same stability caveats as entity-print - never a guaranteed API (G1 report 1.8)
Archive at /zr-downloads: 10 dated complete-ZR PDF snapshots 2024-03-22 to 2026-06-23, 24.32-84.74 MB (E8)

PDF channels: complete ZR /sites/default/files/article/Zoning%20Resolution%20Complete.pdf (102,565,724 B HEAD-verified); per-article (Article I = 3,170,980 B); per-chapter (Chapter 1 = 149,858 B); per-section on-the-fly /entityprint/pdf/node/{nid} -> /print/pdf/node/{nid} (undocumented entity_print route; 200/44,977 B for 11-02, 504 for 12-10) (E6, E10)

Search at /search with parameter search_term (custom nyczr_search_global_search_form module); unknown parameters are silently ignored and yield '0 results' - connector must use search_term and a positive-control query (E13)
fields_available.api_surface_absent: Verified absent 2026-07-16: /jsonapi 404; /node/{nid}?_format=json 406; /sitemap.xml 404; no ZR-text dataset in the NYC Open Data catalog (Socrata catalog search q='zoning resolution' returned only geodata products) (E2, E5)

terms_usage_notes: Official disclaimer (verbatim, E12): the ZR site "IS DISTRIBUTED AND TRANSMITTED WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED"; "DCP IS NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES"; "DCP RESERVES THE RIGHT TO REVISE THIS DISCLAIMER AT ANY TIME AND WITHOUT NOTICE." The disclaimer does NOT state that the online version is the official legal text (OQ-11 - authoritative-publication question needs qualified-human review before 'verified' labeling policy). Robots.txt is standard Drupal (no content-path disallows) (E5).
connector_implementation: PLAN ONLY (M1-T004 is research): (1) zr-portal-crawler Render worker crawls article->chapter->section tree (no sitemap), stores raw HTML per section in Supabase Storage with retrieval timestamp + banner date + per-section Last Amended -> legal_sections / legal_section_versions; re-crawl triggered by banner-date change or new /recently-adopted entry. (2) zr-snapshot-archiver downloads each new dated complete-PDF snapshot (72-85 MB) on a worker into source-pdfs. (3) Per-release HTML-vs-chapter-PDF sampling cross-check. See docs/research/zoning-resolution-2026-07-16.md sections 6-7 for the 14-fixture contract-test pack.
latest_source_version: Text current through City Council adoptions of 2026-05-20 (homepage banner, E3). Newest frozen snapshot: AllArticles_23Jun2026_compressed_0.pdf (75,589,847 B, Last-Modified 2026-06-24, E8). Current complete PDF regenerated 2026-06-23 (E6). PDF file dates are generation timestamps; the banner date is the legal-currency version label.
health_status: healthy_observed — all channels returned expected responses on 2026-07-16 except the undocumented per-section print-PDF route (504 on the oversized 12-10 node, E10) and 403-bound www.nyc.gov companion pages (E17)
known_limitations: No API/JSON channel and no Open Data dataset of the text - HTML crawling is the only per-section machine channel (tier 4)
No official statement that the portal text is the legally authoritative version; disclaimer warns changes 'MAY OR MAY NOT BE IMMEDIATELY REFLECTED' (E12)
'Last Amended' is not officially defined as adoption vs effective date (OQ-3)
Historical snapshots only reach back to 2024-03-22; pre-2024 text (incl. pre-City-of-Yes states) has no verified official channel (OQ-4)
No per-section version history or diff channel; /recently-adopted entries are stubs without amendment text (E11)

Amendments are integrated inline without program branding - full-text search for 'city of yes' returns 0 results. [RESOLVED AT G1 - C2]: CoY-HO adoption officially confirmed (N 240290 ZRY, adopted by City Council 2024-12-05) via the portal's own amendment popup + council.nyc.gov press release 2024-12-05 + Legistar LU 0181-2024 (OQ-2 resolved; G1 report 1.7)
Search silently ignores unknown parameters and reports 0 results (parameter trap, E13)
entity-print per-section PDFs are undocumented and 504 on large nodes - never a dependency (OQ-6)

OWNER DIRECTIVE 2026-07-16: the amendment-history AJAX endpoint (C1) is monitored OPTIONAL ENRICHMENT with graceful fallback only - the amendment/legal-text truth chain remains the server-rendered section HTML (Last Amended stamps) + /recently-adopted feed + dated complete-PDF snapshots; the undocumented endpoint must never become the sole source of amendment or legal-text truth, and its availability must be health-monitored like entity-print

Two hostnames serve the site (zoningresolution.planning.nyc.gov / zr.planning.nyc.gov). [NARROWED AT G1 - C9]: each hostname serves a self-referencing Link: <https://{host}/node/54>; rel="canonical" header - no cross-host canonical; hostname choice is a connector-config decision with the alias recorded (OQ-7; G1 report 1.1)
Archive PDF filename conventions drifted over time (two naming styles observed) - manifest parsing must be tolerant (E8)

WebFetch-summarizer URL error observed during research: the summarizer transferred the real /index.php/ HTML-route prefix (present in the theme's site-logo/footer links; /index.php/{alias} is a valid Drupal front-controller route returning 200 for HTML pages) onto the archive-PDF hrefs, where it 404s for /sites/default/files/ static assets ([CORRECTED AT G1 - C3] - original 'hallucinated prefix' diagnosis was imprecise). All URL claims in fixtures must be raw-capture based (E7, report section 5.5; G1 report 1.5)
Table markup conventions not yet inspected (OQ-8); section 12-01(b) requires text to control over tables in any extraction pipeline (E13)
fallback_source: Per-article and per-chapter PDFs on the same portal (/sites/default/files/article/{node}/... , HEAD-verified, E6) and dated complete-PDF snapshots (/zr-downloads, E8) - same publisher, so this is a channel fallback, not an independent-source fallback. www.nyc.gov DCP zoning pages are 403 to non-browser clients (E17). No independent official mirror of the ZR text was found. [CORRECTED AT G1 - C2]: for amendment-ADOPTION records, council.nyc.gov and legistar.council.nyc.gov were verified automation-accessible official channels (no bot wall) - the official fallback for adoption records; the CPC report chain a030-cpc.nyc.gov/html/cpc/report.aspx?num={ULURP} 302s to a www1.nyc.gov PDF that is 403-bound (browser needed) (G1 report 1.7-1.8).
open_questions: OQ-1: '11 Appendices' count vs observed letters B-K; no Appendix A (/appendix-a 404); reconciliation not officially stated

OQ-2 [RESOLVED AT G1 - C2]: City of Yes for Housing Opportunity (N 240290 ZRY) adopted by City Council 2024-12-05 - confirmed via the portal's amendment popup + council.nyc.gov/press/2024/12/05/2761/ + Legistar LU 0181-2024 (G1 report 1.7)

OQ-3 [NARROWED AT G1 - C1]: 'Last Amended' semantics - the amendment popup officially labels per-amendment dates 'Effective Date'; adoption-vs-effective remains undiscriminated where the two coincide (G1 report 1.8)
OQ-4: official channel for pre-2024-03-22 historical ZR text
OQ-5: chapter gaps (Article I lacks chapter 4) - reserved or repealed; full 14-article chapter inventory pending
OQ-6: entity-print route stability and DCP intent

OQ-7 [NARROWED AT G1 - C9]: canonical hostname - each host serves a self-referencing Link rel="canonical" header (no cross-host canonical); hostname choice is connector config with the alias recorded (G1 report 1.1)
OQ-8: table markup conventions (needed before M3 table ingestion; fixture ZR-F6)

OQ-9 [RESOLVED AT G1 - C1]: nyczr_amendment_popup is backed by GET /ajax/get/amendment/section/{sectionEntityId}?_wrapper_format=drupal_ajax (+ X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest) -> per-section amendment-history JSON (6-column table: Effective Date | ULURP/CPC Report | Project Name | Action | Notes | Description); undocumented, entity-print-grade stability caveats (G1 report 1.8)
OQ-10 [PARTIALLY RESOLVED AT G1 - C5]: /recently-adopted feed depth = 31 pages (0-30, 'Go to last page' href); entry mutability still open
OQ-11: which publication is legally authoritative (needs qualified-human/legal review)
OQ-12: content identity of the 102.6 MB complete PDF vs the 75.6 MB compressed snapshot
